title: Non-escape of Mass for Arithmetic Quantum Limits on Hyperbolic 4-Manifolds
speaker: Alexandre de Faveri
abstract:
The Arithmetic Quantum Unique Ergodicity (AQUE) conjecture predicts that the L2 mass of Hecke-Maass cusp forms on an arithmetic hyperbolic manifold becomes equidistributed as the Laplace eigenvalue grows. If the underlying manifold is non-compact, mass could “escape to infinity”. This possibility was ruled out by Soundararajan for arithmetic surfaces, which when combined with celebrated work of Lindenstrauss completed the proof of AQUE for surfaces. 
We establish non-escape of mass for Hecke-Maass cusp forms on a congruence quotient of hyperbolic 4-space. Unlike in the setting of hyperbolic 2- or 3-manifolds (for which AQUE has been proved), the number of terms in the Hecke relations is unbounded, which prevents us from naively applying Cauchy-Schwarz. We instead view the isometry group as a group of quaternionic matrices, and rely on non-commutative unique factorization along with certain structural features of the Hecke action. Joint work with Zvi Shem-Tov.
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Arithmetic QUE framework and four-dimensional theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up Laplace eigenfunctions and quantum unique ergodicity on negatively curved manifolds, specializes to congruence quotients with Hecke eigenbases, reviews known two- and three-dimensional arithmetic cases, and states the main result that arithmetic quantum limits on the relevant hyperbolic 4-manifolds cannot lose mass in the cusp.

proof_stages.label: Rank-one mechanism: comparison inequality implies non-escape
proof_stages.summary: Using the SL2(Z) cusp as the model, Hecke operators are presented as devices for pushing mass down from the cusp, and Soundararajan's uniform comparison inequality for Fourier-coefficient sums is shown via Parseval and positivity to imply non-escape of mass.

proof_stages.label: Four-dimensional Fourier expansion and target comparison bound
proof_stages.summary: For the hyperbolic 4-manifold quotient, the speaker introduces the classical Fourier expansion and formulates the analogous uniform comparison inequality whose proof will give the desired non-escape theorem.

proof_stages.label: Soundararajan amplification dichotomy
proof_stages.summary: The SL2 comparison inequality is explained by splitting Fourier coefficients according to whether they have many good prime factors, amplifying the low-multiplicity part with many Hecke operators, and controlling the highly divisible part recursively by the Hecke relations.

proof_stages.label: Hecke algebra and quaternionic coefficient relations in dimension four
proof_stages.summary: The four-dimensional case is reduced to explicit Hecke relations involving two algebraically independent Hecke operators and a third auxiliary one, with Fourier coefficients indexed by traceless quaternions and acted on by conjugation by norm-p quaternions.

proof_stages.label: Weighted Cauchy control of quaternionic multiplicities
proof_stages.summary: Because the relevant Hecke relation contains about p conjugation terms, naive Cauchy--Schwarz would lose too much, so the proof detects an 'increasing' set of quaternionic factors and inserts weights that keep the dangerous multiplicities uniformly bounded.

proof_stages.label: Switching Hecke operators for the second eigenvalue regime
proof_stages.summary: When amplification must use the second Hecke eigenvalue, new high-multiplicity terms appear, and the proof handles them by rewriting the problematic conjugation packet through a different Hecke relation at beta p or p^2 beta, with separate treatments according to the eigenvalue size.

proof_stages.label: Recursive closure of the comparison inequality
proof_stages.summary: The weighted and switched Hecke estimates are assembled into a recursive inequality for the coefficient-sum comparison function, yielding the required uniform logarithmic bound and hence the non-escape theorem.

examples.name: Bianchi modular 3-manifold
examples.note: The quotient SL2(C)/SL2(Z[i]) is mentioned as the three-dimensional analogue where non-escape and arithmetic quantum unique ergodicity are already known; it contextualizes the four-dimensional theorem rather than generating a proof idea.

examples.name: Compact arithmetic hyperbolic 4-quotients without hypersurfaces
examples.note: An audience question asks whether compact SO(4,1) arithmetic quotients lacking totally geodesic codimension-one submanifolds would already avoid the remaining QUE obstruction; this clarifies the role of those submanifolds in the theorem's context.
examples.engagement_reason: compact arithmetic hyperbolic 4-quotients without totally geodesic hypersurfaces are discussed hypothetically in a question, with only general comments about possible sources

examples.name: GL3 higher-rank test case
examples.note: In Q&A, GL3 is cited as a case where an analogous comparison inequality can be proved but does not imply non-escape because the relevant incomplete Eisenstein series lacks uniform boundedness, illustrating a limitation of the rank-one reduction.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies the comparison-inequality strategy to the GL3 case and reasons through why it does not yield non-escape of mass, via the incomplete Eisenstein series obstruction

passes.reason: standard GL3 higher-rank case used to show the comparison-inequality strategy does not by itself imply non-escape of mass
